Now, this is what we call anchoring. So when you say 17,000 to 32,000, you're creating this huge number in people's mind. So then you come out with a number that's, in this case, $1,000. That all of a sudden looks a lot lower because I said it right after 17 to 32,000. Now, if I had said, if I talked about, you know, in the past, I sold, you know, I've mostly focused on, you know, selling e-books for $47, but I decided I'd go, you know, those books, those e-books just weren't, you know, all that in depth. And I decided to give you some really in-depth interactive training. So it's going to be more expensive. It's going to cost $987. When you first say $47, then say $987, it's nowhere near as effective as saying $32,000 and then saying $987. So this was very much not an accident. Anytime you can anchor pricing, then you want to anchor a higher price. So what if you are a guitar teacher and you have a course that you're going to try to sell for $297? You're going to do your seed launch for $287 for a course on how to play guitar. You've never sold a product before. However, for years you've taught lessons. One thing you could do is you could offer $9.97, like in this case $9.97 with three payments of $347, and then if it wasn't going selling as well as you hoped, you could come back and do a six payment plan, so a more generous payment plan. Do you cancel class or leave cart open longer? You know, the first time I did one of these for Franklin's Formula, I got six people. So I got six people and then I calmed in about 30 or 40 friends of mine that I knew that just wanted the stuff. They wanted the training. So they were friends of mine. I didn't even try to sell them. I just calmed them in. That's how I got up to somewhere around 40 people. Question, any reason to pay more for go-to webinar versus instant tell seminar? Question from Michael. You know, we've used instant tell seminar for years. This is the first time, literally, Sea Launch Deep Dive is the first time I've used go-to webinar. And it costs a lot more than instant tell seminar. But frankly, I am liking go-to webinar quite a bit. I'm going to use it in the future. So I mean, if the pricing, you know, it's a big difference. It's like, I think instant tell somewhere it's like 50 bucks a month or something. And go-to webinar is like hundreds. So it's a big difference. But you know, you could do, you can use go-to webinar, I think for a month. You know, and test it out for a month, it's like a refund. Or you could probably get your whole Sea Launch done within a couple months. So, you know, then, you know, it's not like you have to keep it forever. So frankly, I'm liking go-to webinar just because of the additional interactivity. People being able to type in questions like this doesn't happen in instant tell seminar. There is a way for them to answer, ask questions, but it's a lot more cumbersome. And it's less fluid in real time.

## [1:12:55 - 1:15:47]

Question, can you use Google Hangouts? I suspect that you can. I haven't. Yeah, it's just not something I've tested. There's a lot of people asking about different services. What about real talk? What about webinar express? I don't know. You know, there's more services out there than I could possibly test. And so, yeah, I mean, we've used instant tell seminar. We've used Accu-conference. We've used go-to webinar. We've used other stuff. You know, there's threeconferencecall.com. You can use a lot of those we talked about in the earlier module, the last module, where I was actually talking about creating the product. So, lots of different options you can use. And they might work. And this is just the one I'm using here. And so far, I'm frankly pretty happy. Question, what are your thoughts on doing three segments instead of five? So, in other words, three calls to three modules instead of five? Totally fine. Five tends to be a pretty good number, but if you can do it in three and deliver value in three, absolutely. You know, I would, and the other thing too is, like, I always want to do at least one bonus call. So, you know, I might do three and then do a bonus call. But yeah, you can do, if it takes you six, fine, do six. But, you know, five tends to be a good number. And generally when you're teaching, it's better just to give people a real specific. So, that's why I've been saying five. But if you think three works for you, then, yeah, go for three. Okay, folks, my voice is absolutely starting to go here. I don't think that can happen. But, yeah, this is a great interaction. Thanks for hanging in there with me. Thanks for all your questions, your feedback throughout.
